Littérature Africaine.question: Which Nigerian writer won the Nobel Prize for Literature in 1986?
Littérature Africaine.explanation: Wole Soyinka is the first African to receive the Nobel Prize for Literature.

Littérature Africaine.question: Who wrote 'The Ambiguous Adventure'?
Littérature Africaine.explanation: 'The Ambiguous Adventure' (1961) is a novel by Cheikh Hamidou Kane about African identity.

Littérature Africaine.question: Which Malian writer is known for 'Such a Long Letter'?
Littérature Africaine.explanation: Mariama Bâ (Senegalese) wrote this epistolary novel about the condition of women in Africa.

Littérature Africaine.question: Which Nigerian author popularized the term “Afro-feminism” in her essays?
Littérature Africaine.explanation: Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie is a major voice in contemporary African literature.

Littérature Africaine.question: Which Chinua Achebe novel is considered a classic of African literature?
Littérature Africaine.explanation: “Things Fall Apart” (1958) is translated into French under the title “Le monde s’colfe”.

Littérature Africaine.question: Which international literary prize rewards a French-speaking author each year?
Littérature Africaine.explanation: The Prix Goncourt is one of the most prestigious French-speaking literary prizes.

Littérature Africaine.question: Which Senegalese poet co-founded the Négritude movement?
Littérature Africaine.explanation: Senghor, Césaire and Damas are the founding figures of Négritude.

Littérature Africaine.question: Which Spanish painter co-founded Cubism with Braque?
Littérature Africaine.explanation: Picasso and Braque developed Cubism in the early 20th century.

Littérature Africaine.question: Which Paris museum houses the Mona Lisa?
Littérature Africaine.explanation: Leonardo da Vinci's Mona Lisa is displayed at the Louvre.

Littérature Africaine.question: Who composed The Four Seasons?
Littérature Africaine.explanation: Antonio Vivaldi composed The Four Seasons.

Littérature Africaine.question: Who wrote Les Misérables?
Littérature Africaine.explanation: Les Misérables (1862) is a novel by Victor Hugo.

Littérature Africaine.question: Which architectural style characterizes Egypt's pyramids?
Littérature Africaine.explanation: The pyramids are monuments of ancient Egypt.

Littérature Africaine.question: Which film festival is held each year in Cannes?
Littérature Africaine.explanation: Cannes is one of the world's most prestigious festivals.

Littérature Africaine.question: Which dance originated in Argentina?
Littérature Africaine.explanation: Tango originated in Buenos Aires / Montevideo.

Littérature Africaine.question: Who sculpted The Thinker?
Littérature Africaine.explanation: The Thinker is a sculpture by Auguste Rodin.

Littérature Africaine.question: Which Malian craft is famous for mud-cloth textiles?
Littérature Africaine.explanation: Bogolan is a traditional Malian dyed cloth.

Littérature Africaine.question: Which Leonardo da Vinci work depicts a biblical meal?
Littérature Africaine.explanation: The Last Supper depicts Christ's final meal.

Littérature Africaine.question: Which art movement is associated with Claude Monet?
Littérature Africaine.explanation: Monet is a major Impressionist figure.

Littérature Africaine.question: Which instrument is central to West African kora music?
Littérature Africaine.explanation: The kora is a West African harp-lute.

Littérature Africaine.question: Who wrote Romeo and Juliet?
Littérature Africaine.explanation: Romeo and Juliet is a Shakespeare tragedy.

Littérature Africaine.question: Which New York museum is famous for modern art?
Littérature Africaine.explanation: The Museum of Modern Art (MoMA) is in New York.

Littérature Africaine.question: Which color dominates Picasso's Blue Period?
Littérature Africaine.explanation: Picasso's Blue Period uses mostly blue tones.

Cinéma Africain.question: What is the name of the largest African film festival?
Cinéma Africain.explanation: The FESPACO (Ouagadougou Pan-African Cinema Festival) is held every two years in Burkina Faso.

Cinéma Africain.question: Which Senegalese director is considered the father of African cinema?
Cinéma Africain.explanation: Ousmane Sembène (1923-2007) is considered the father of African cinema.

Cinéma Africain.question: Which Abderrahmane Sissako film was nominated for the Oscars in 2015?
Cinéma Africain.explanation: “Timbuktu” by Abderrahmane Sissako was nominated for an Oscar for best foreign film.

Cinéma Africain.question: Which African film industry produces the most films per year?
Cinéma Africain.explanation: Nollywood is one of the most prolific film industries in the world.

Cinéma Africain.question: Which Malian director won the Grand Prix at the Cannes Film Festival in 1987?
Cinéma Africain.explanation: Souleymane Cissé won the Grand Prix for “Yeelen” in 1987.

Cinéma Africain.question: Which streaming platform has strongly developed African series since 2020?
Cinéma Africain.explanation: Netflix has invested in original African productions such as “Blood & Water” and “Aníkúlápó”.

Art Contemporain.question: Which Malian artist is famous for his photographs?
Art Contemporain.explanation: Malick Sidibé immortalized the Malian youth of the 60s and 70s in Bamako.

Art Contemporain.question: Which Beninese artist exhibited at the Musée du Quai Branly with emblematic sculptures?
Art Contemporain.explanation: Romuald Hazoumè uses recycled materials to question contemporary society.

Art Contemporain.question: Which African art biennial is held in Dakar?
Art Contemporain.explanation: Dak'Art is the Biennale of Contemporary African Art, created in 1990.

Art Contemporain.question: Which Senegalese photographer is famous for his studio portraits in Dakar?
Art Contemporain.explanation: Seydou Keïta is recognized worldwide for his black and white portraits in Bamako and Dakar.

Musique Africaine Moderne.question: Which Nigerian musical genre experienced a global explosion in the 2020s?
Musique Africaine Moderne.explanation: Afrobeats, worn by Burna Boy, Wizkid and Rema, has become a global phenomenon.

Musique Africaine Moderne.question: Which Malian artist is nicknamed “the Golden Voice of Africa”?
Musique Africaine Moderne.explanation: Salif Keita is a legend of Malian and world music.

Musique Africaine Moderne.question: Which music festival has been held every year in Mali since 2005?
Musique Africaine Moderne.explanation: The Niger Festival in Ségou celebrates the culture and music of Mali.

Musique Africaine Moderne.question: Which application has transformed African musical discovery in 2020-2025?
Musique Africaine Moderne.explanation: Streaming platforms have amplified the visibility of African artists around the world.

Patrimoine et UNESCO.question: Which Malian city is listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site?
Patrimoine et UNESCO.explanation: Timbuktu, Djenné and the Niger Loop have been listed as UNESCO heritage sites.

Patrimoine et UNESCO.question: Which earth mosque in Mali is famous for its unique architecture?
Patrimoine et UNESCO.explanation: The Djenné mosque is the largest raw earth construction in the world.

Patrimoine et UNESCO.question: Which museum in Dakar celebrates black civilizations?
Patrimoine et UNESCO.explanation: Inaugurated in 2018, this museum highlights the art and history of black civilizations.
